product_name: Keysight CyPerf (Note: Document text primarily describes "Keysight Embedded Security Testbench" and general PQC validation services; "CyPerf" appears only in the user prompt context, not explicitly defined as a specific product in the provided text).
product_brief: Comprehensive portfolio to validate and harden embedded systems against side-channel and fault-injection attacks for post-quantum cryptography.
pqc_capability_description: Provides testing solutions and expert services to validate PQC implementations on resource-constrained hardware. Supports validation from design to deployment, focusing on side-channel analysis (SCA) and fault injection (FI) to detect key-dependent leakage and fault-based key recovery. Helps align with standards like Common Criteria and EUCC.
crypto_primitives: RSA, ECC (mentioned as current algorithms at risk); PQC algorithms mentioned generally but specific names (e.g., CRYSTALS-Kyber) are not stated.
architecture_type: Hardware security testing setup (Embedded Security Testbench), software tools, and services.
infrastructure_layer: Hardware, Network (implied via embedded/IoT context), Security Stack
fips_validated: No mention of FIPS validation status.
primary_platforms: Embedded systems, IoT devices, secure elements, firmware, secure boot.
target_industries: Payments, automotive, critical infrastructure, government, defense.
regulatory_status: Mentions alignment with Common Criteria and EUCC; no specific licenses or charters held by the product itself are stated.
pqc_roadmap_details: Supports organizations in planning compliant, secure implementations and migrating to quantum-safe algorithms; mentions 15–20 year timeline for CRQCs.
signature_schemes: Not stated (General mention of PQC signature validation, no specific schemes listed).

product_brief: Platform supporting evaluation of PQC algorithms against side-channel leakage and fault injection vulnerabilities.
pqc_capability_description: Supports evaluation of ML-DSA, with pre-release capabilities for ML-KEM and hash-based signatures (LMS, XMSS, SLH-DSA). Focuses on assessing robustness against side-channel leakage and fault injection in hardware, software, and hybrid implementations.
architecture_type: Evaluation platform for cryptographic implementations (hardware, software, hybrid)
primary_platforms: Embedded systems, IoT devices, government-grade secure communications (evaluation context)
pqc_roadmap_details: Supports evaluation of NIST-selected algorithms including ML-DSA, ML-KEM, and hash-based signatures; pre-release capabilities available for ML-KEM and hash-based schemes.
